passed_checks.check_name: Ensure the base image uses a non latest version tag
code_block: FROM python:3.13-slim

passed_checks.check_name: Ensure From Alias are unique for multistage builds.
code_block:
FROM python:3.13-slim

WORKDIR /app

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y \
    libpq-dev \
    gcc \
    libpango-1.0-0 \
    libcairo2 \
    libgdk-pixbuf-2.0-0 \
    libffi-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

RUN useradd --no-create-home --shell /bin/false appuser \
    && chown -R appuser:appuser /app
USER appuser

EXPOSE 8000
CMD ["uvicorn", "app.main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]

#      JWT is HS256-signed with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET so

cp .github/workflows/zap_auth_hook.py "${REPORT_DIR}/zap_auth_hook.py"

  -v "$(pwd)/${REPORT_DIR}:/zap/wrk" \

    -f openapi \
    -J "zap_${TS}.json" \

code_block:
lint-and-test:
    name: Lint & Test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: findone
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: findone
          POSTGRES_DB: findone_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: .nvmrc
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/package-lock.json

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
          cache: pip
          cache-dependency-path: backend/requirements*.txt

      - name: Install backend dependencies
        run: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt -r backend/requirements-dev.txt

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm ci

      - name: Backend — pytest
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test
          SUPABASE_URL: ""
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY: ""
          SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET: ""
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ""
          JSEARCH_API_KEY: ""
        run: pytest backend/tests/ -v --tb=short

      - name: Backend — flake8
        run: flake8 backend/app/ --max-line-length=100

      - name: Backend — mypy
        run: mypy backend/app/ --ignore-missing-imports

      - name: Frontend — vitest
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm run test:run

      - name: Frontend — eslint
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm run lint

  security-scans:
    name: Security Scans
    needs: lint-and-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging

    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: findone
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: findone
          POSTGRES_DB: findone_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: .nvmrc
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/package-lock.json

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
          cache: pip
          cache-dependency-path: backend/requirements*.txt

      - name: Install Python dependencies
        run: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt bandit semgrep pip-audit

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm ci

      - name: Create report directory
        id: report_dir
        run: |
          TIMESTAMP=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S')
          REPORT_DIR="security-reports/${TIMESTAMP}"
          mkdir -p "${REPORT_DIR}"
          echo "dir=${REPORT_DIR}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ts=${TIMESTAMP}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: TruffleHog
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          pip install trufflehog3 2>/dev/null || pip install trufflehog 2>/dev/null || true
          trufflehog git file://. --only-verified --json \
            > "${REPORT_DIR}/trufflehog_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Real findings contain "DetectorName"; log lines contain "level" — grep distinguishes them
          if grep -q '"DetectorName"' "${REPORT_DIR}/trufflehog_${TS}.json" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::TruffleHog found verified secrets in git history."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: pip-audit
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          # Always save the JSON report for artifact upload
          pip-audit -r backend/requirements.txt -f json \
            -o "${REPORT_DIR}/pip-audit_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Block if any vulnerability found (pip-audit exits 1 when vulns exist)
          pip-audit -r backend/requirements.txt 2>&1 || {
            echo "::error::pip-audit found vulnerabilities in Python dependencies."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: npm audit
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        working-directory: frontend
        run: |
          # Always save the JSON report for artifact upload
          npm audit --json > "../${REPORT_DIR}/npm-audit_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Block on CRITICAL severity only
          npm audit --audit-level=critical || {
            echo "::error::npm audit found CRITICAL vulnerabilities in JS dependencies."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Bandit
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          # Always save the full JSON report for artifact upload
          bandit -r backend/app/ -f json \
            -o "${REPORT_DIR}/bandit_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Block on HIGH severity + MEDIUM confidence (exit code 1 when issues found)
          bandit -r backend/app/ \
            --severity-level high \
            --confidence-level medium \
            --quiet 2>&1 || {
            echo "::error::Bandit found HIGH severity issues in Python code."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Semgrep
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          semgrep scan --config=auto --json \
            --output="${REPORT_DIR}/semgrep_${TS}.json" \
            backend/ frontend/src/ 2>&1 || true

      - name: Checkov
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          pip install checkov
          checkov -d . --output json \
            > "${REPORT_DIR}/checkov_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true

      - name: Run Alembic migrations for ZAP test DB
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test
        run: |
          cd backend
          alembic upgrade head

      - name: OWASP ZAP baseline scan (authenticated)
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          JSEARCH_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.JSEARCH_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          docker pull ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable 2>/dev/null || true
          # Start the backend with REAL Supabase secrets — auth actually
          # works, so ZAP (with the service_role JWT below) can reach

          # cd into backend/ so `from app.config` resolves.
          pip install -r backend/requirements.txt 2>/dev/null || true
          (
            cd backend
            DATABASE_URL="postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test" \

          if ! curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "::error::backend failed to start — ZAP would scan nothing"

          chmod 777 "${REPORT_DIR}"
          cp .github/workflows/zap_auth_hook.py "${REPORT_DIR}/zap_auth_hook.py"

          # zap-api-scan.py consumes FastAPI's /openapi.json — every

            ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable \
            zap-api-scan.py \
              -t http://localhost:8000/openapi.json \
              -f openapi \

          # authenticated endpoints instead of being bounced with 401.

name: Security Scans
    needs: lint-and-test
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    environment: staging

    services:
      postgres:
        image: postgres:16
        env:
          POSTGRES_USER: findone
          POSTGRES_PASSWORD: findone
          POSTGRES_DB: findone_test
        ports:
          - 5432:5432
        options: >-
          --health-cmd pg_isready
          --health-interval 10s
          --health-timeout 5s
          --health-retries 5

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version-file: .nvmrc
          cache: npm
          cache-dependency-path: frontend/package-lock.json

      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.13'
          cache: pip
          cache-dependency-path: backend/requirements*.txt

      - name: Install Python dependencies
        run: pip install -r backend/requirements.txt bandit semgrep pip-audit

      - name: Install frontend dependencies
        working-directory: frontend
        run: npm ci

      - name: Create report directory
        id: report_dir
        run: |
          TIMESTAMP=$(date +'%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M-%S')
          REPORT_DIR="security-reports/${TIMESTAMP}"
          mkdir -p "${REPORT_DIR}"
          echo "dir=${REPORT_DIR}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "ts=${TIMESTAMP}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: TruffleHog
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          pip install trufflehog3 2>/dev/null || pip install trufflehog 2>/dev/null || true
          trufflehog git file://. --only-verified --json \
            > "${REPORT_DIR}/trufflehog_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Real findings contain "DetectorName"; log lines contain "level" — grep distinguishes them
          if grep -q '"DetectorName"' "${REPORT_DIR}/trufflehog_${TS}.json" 2>/dev/null; then
            echo "::error::TruffleHog found verified secrets in git history."
            exit 1
          fi

      - name: pip-audit
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          # Always save the JSON report for artifact upload
          pip-audit -r backend/requirements.txt -f json \
            -o "${REPORT_DIR}/pip-audit_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Block if any vulnerability found (pip-audit exits 1 when vulns exist)
          pip-audit -r backend/requirements.txt 2>&1 || {
            echo "::error::pip-audit found vulnerabilities in Python dependencies."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: npm audit
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        working-directory: frontend
        run: |
          # Always save the JSON report for artifact upload
          npm audit --json > "../${REPORT_DIR}/npm-audit_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Block on CRITICAL severity only
          npm audit --audit-level=critical || {
            echo "::error::npm audit found CRITICAL vulnerabilities in JS dependencies."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Bandit
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          # Always save the full JSON report for artifact upload
          bandit -r backend/app/ -f json \
            -o "${REPORT_DIR}/bandit_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true
          # Block on HIGH severity + MEDIUM confidence (exit code 1 when issues found)
          bandit -r backend/app/ \
            --severity-level high \
            --confidence-level medium \
            --quiet 2>&1 || {
            echo "::error::Bandit found HIGH severity issues in Python code."
            exit 1
          }

      - name: Semgrep
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          semgrep scan --config=auto --json \
            --output="${REPORT_DIR}/semgrep_${TS}.json" \
            backend/ frontend/src/ 2>&1 || true

      - name: Checkov
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
        run: |
          pip install checkov
          checkov -d . --output json \
            > "${REPORT_DIR}/checkov_${TS}.json" 2>&1 || true

      - name: Run Alembic migrations for ZAP test DB
        env:
          DATABASE_URL: postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test
        run: |
          cd backend
          alembic upgrade head

      - name: OWASP ZAP baseline scan (authenticated)
        env:
          REPORT_DIR: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}
          TS: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
          SUPABASE_URL: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_URL }}
          SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET }}
          SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY: ${{ secrets.SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY }}
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY }}
          JSEARCH_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.JSEARCH_API_KEY }}
        run: |
          docker pull ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable 2>/dev/null || true
          # Start the backend with REAL Supabase secrets — auth actually
          # works, so ZAP (with the service_role JWT below) can reach
          # authenticated endpoints instead of being bounced with 401.
          # cd into backend/ so `from app.config` resolves.
          pip install -r backend/requirements.txt 2>/dev/null || true
          (
            cd backend
            DATABASE_URL="postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test" \
              uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 \
              > /tmp/uvicorn.log 2>&1 &
          )
          for i in $(seq 1 20); do
            if curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
              echo "backend up after ${i}s"
              break
            fi
            sleep 1
          done
          if ! curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            echo "::error::backend failed to start — ZAP would scan nothing"
            echo "--- uvicorn.log ---"; cat /tmp/uvicorn.log || true
            exit 1
          fi

          # Prep the ZAP working directory on the host:
          #  (1) chmod 777 so the container's non-root 'zap' user can write
          #  (2) copy the auth hook into it; the hook reads
          #      ZAP_AUTH_TOKEN and adds Authorization: Bearer <token>
          #      on every request via ZAP's replacer API. Service-role
          #      JWT is HS256-signed with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET so
          #      backend/app/services/auth.py accepts it through the
          #      symmetric path.
          chmod 777 "${REPORT_DIR}"
          cp .github/workflows/zap_auth_hook.py "${REPORT_DIR}/zap_auth_hook.py"

          # zap-api-scan.py consumes FastAPI's /openapi.json — every
          # declared route is enumerated and scanned with the Bearer
          # token injected by the hook. zap-baseline.py's HTML spider
          # can't discover REST endpoints, which was capping us at ~3
          # routes (/health plus redirects).
          docker run --rm --network host \
            -v "$(pwd)/${REPORT_DIR}:/zap/wrk" \
            -e ZAP_AUTH_TOKEN="${SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY}" \
            ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable \
            zap-api-scan.py \
              -t http://localhost:8000/openapi.json \
              -f openapi \
              -J "zap_${TS}.json" \
              --hook=/zap/wrk/zap_auth_hook.py \
              -d 2>&1 | tee /tmp/zap_output.txt || true

      - name: Upload security artifacts
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: security-report-${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
          path: ${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.dir }}/
          retention-days: 30

      - name: Generate SBOM
        if: always()
        uses: anchore/sbom-action@v0
        with:
          path: .
          format: spdx-json
          artifact-name: sbom.spdx.json

      - name: Upload SBOM artifact
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: sbom-${{ steps.report_dir.outputs.ts }}
          path: sbom.spdx.json
          retention-days: 30

results_configuration.name: OWASP ZAP baseline scan (authenticated)
results_configuration.run:
docker pull ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable 2>/dev/null || true
# Start the backend with REAL Supabase secrets — auth actually
# works, so ZAP (with the service_role JWT below) can reach
# authenticated endpoints instead of being bounced with 401.
# cd into backend/ so `from app.config` resolves.
pip install -r backend/requirements.txt 2>/dev/null || true
(
  cd backend
  DATABASE_URL="postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test" \
    uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 \
    > /tmp/uvicorn.log 2>&1 &
)
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  if curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "backend up after ${i}s"
    break
  fi
  sleep 1
done
if ! curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "::error::backend failed to start — ZAP would scan nothing"
  echo "--- uvicorn.log ---"; cat /tmp/uvicorn.log || true
  exit 1
fi

# Prep the ZAP working directory on the host:
#  (1) chmod 777 so the container's non-root 'zap' user can write
#  (2) copy the auth hook into it; the hook reads
#      ZAP_AUTH_TOKEN and adds Authorization: Bearer <token>
#      on every request via ZAP's replacer API. Service-role
#      JWT is HS256-signed with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET so
#      backend/app/services/auth.py accepts it through the
#      symmetric path.
chmod 777 "${REPORT_DIR}"
cp .github/workflows/zap_auth_hook.py "${REPORT_DIR}/zap_auth_hook.py"

# zap-api-scan.py consumes FastAPI's /openapi.json — every
# declared route is enumerated and scanned with the Bearer
# token injected by the hook. zap-baseline.py's HTML spider
# can't discover REST endpoints, which was capping us at ~3
# routes (/health plus redirects).
docker run --rm --network host \
  -v "$(pwd)/${REPORT_DIR}:/zap/wrk" \
  -e ZAP_AUTH_TOKEN="${SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY}" \
  ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable \
  zap-api-scan.py \
    -t http://localhost:8000/openapi.json \
    -f openapi \
    -J "zap_${TS}.json" \
    --hook=/zap/wrk/zap_auth_hook.py \
    -d 2>&1 | tee /tmp/zap_output.txt || true

results_configuration.name: OWASP ZAP baseline scan (authenticated)
results_configuration.run:
docker pull ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable 2>/dev/null || true
# Start the backend with REAL Supabase secrets — auth actually
# works, so ZAP (with the service_role JWT below) can reach
# authenticated endpoints instead of being bounced with 401.
# cd into backend/ so `from app.config` resolves.
pip install -r backend/requirements.txt 2>/dev/null || true
(
  cd backend
  DATABASE_URL="postgresql://findone:findone@localhost:5432/findone_test" \
    uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 \
    > /tmp/uvicorn.log 2>&1 &
)
for i in $(seq 1 20); do
  if curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "backend up after ${i}s"
    break
  fi
  sleep 1
done
if ! curl -sf http://localhost:8000/health >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "::error::backend failed to start — ZAP would scan nothing"
  echo "--- uvicorn.log ---"; cat /tmp/uvicorn.log || true
  exit 1
fi

# Prep the ZAP working directory on the host:
#  (1) chmod 777 so the container's non-root 'zap' user can write
#  (2) copy the auth hook into it; the hook reads
#      ZAP_AUTH_TOKEN and adds Authorization: Bearer <token>
#      on every request via ZAP's replacer API. Service-role
#      JWT is HS256-signed with SUPABASE_JWT_SECRET so
#      backend/app/services/auth.py accepts it through the
#      symmetric path.
chmod 777 "${REPORT_DIR}"
cp .github/workflows/zap_auth_hook.py "${REPORT_DIR}/zap_auth_hook.py"

# zap-api-scan.py consumes FastAPI's /openapi.json — every
# declared route is enumerated and scanned with the Bearer
# token injected by the hook. zap-baseline.py's HTML spider
# can't discover REST endpoints, which was capping us at ~3
# routes (/health plus redirects).
docker run --rm --network host \
  -v "$(pwd)/${REPORT_DIR}:/zap/wrk" \
  -e ZAP_AUTH_TOKEN="${SUPABASE_SERVICE_KEY}" \
  ghcr.io/zaproxy/zaproxy:stable \
  zap-api-scan.py \
    -t http://localhost:8000/openapi.json \
    -f openapi \
    -J "zap_${TS}.json" \
    --hook=/zap/wrk/zap_auth_hook.py \
    -d 2>&1 | tee /tmp/zap_output.txt || true

open-or-update-pr:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Check for existing PR
        id: check_pr
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          PR_NUMBER=$(gh pr list --base main --head dev --state open --json number --jq '.[0].number // empty')
          echo "pr_number=${PR_NUMBER}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Build commit summary
        id: summary
        run: |
          BASE=$(git merge-base origin/main HEAD 2>/dev/null || git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD)
          COMMITS=$(git log "${BASE}..HEAD" --pretty=format:"- %s" | head -20)
          {
            echo 'body<<EOF'
            echo "## Changes"
            echo ""
            echo "${COMMITS}"
            echo ""
            echo "---"
            echo "_Auto-generated by [auto-create-pr workflow](../.github/workflows/auto-create-pr.yml)_"
            echo 'EOF'
          } >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Create PR
        if: steps.check_pr.outputs.pr_number == ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PR_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          gh pr create \
            --base main \
            --head dev \
            --title "chore: merge dev into main $(date +'%Y-%m-%d')" \
            --body "${{ steps.summary.outputs.body }}"

      - name: Update PR description
        if: steps.check_pr.outputs.pr_number != ''
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.PR_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          gh pr edit "${{ steps.check_pr.outputs.pr_number }}" \
            --body "${{ steps.summary.outputs.body }}"
